Adds Black Drop Pouches to vendors and resprites existing Drop Pouches (#9284)

# About the pull request

Ports the PVE drop pouch sprite and resprites the other existing drop
pouch sprites to be around that style.

# Explain why it's good for the game

The current drop pouch sprite didnt fit in for most maps and uniforms,
so respriting it and adding a new black variant was the first thought.
Then i went on to resprite the surgical pouches aswell after realising
theyre pretty old too.

Was asked to port the PVE drop pouch by blundir and went on to sprite
others with it.

# Testing Photographs and Procedure


![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9a8066ac-4ca9-4684-a44c-c5d42ae5e9e2)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/252ebf24-368e-4106-983a-5bee145a2f0f)


# Changelog

:cl:Ansekishoku
add: Added black drop pouches to vendors.
imageadd: Resprited all drop pouch sprites including the surgical drop
pouches and also slightly tweaks item sprites.
/:cl:

CLF survivor changes/nerfs (#9311)

# About the pull request
Adjusts the sentries of CLF survivors to spawn with a random amount of
ammo between 100 - 500 instead of having 99,999 rounds, makes it so only
the SL can drive, bumps up the Engineers skill level and gives the SL
the ability to treat internal bleeding and do basic medical tasks. This
also removes the SL having Endurance 5 and SO equivalent orders, giving
all of them Endurance 1 instead.

# Explain why it's good for the game

CLF survivors are incredibly secure especially on lowpop when they have
infinite ammo turrets surrounding them, enabling them to simply wall off
or fortify the shit out of one specific area.

Endurance 5 also should not be available in regular gameplay to
survivors from what I understand.

Having less people able to drive the van means that it is a risk vs
reward of who you send out rather than having everyone capable of it, so
now you need to keep your synthetic/CLF leader alive.

Overall, brings them more in line with other survivors and stops them
from being as powerful as the actual CLF ERT due to them having an
impact on the gameplay loop.


# Testing Photographs and Procedure

# Changelog
:cl:
balance: CLF squad leader no longer has Endurance 5 or full
construction/engineering skill, now having basic medical and surgical
skills to treat IB. Most CLF roles aside from the squad leader and
synthetic have lost the ability to drive the van. CLF engineer now has
proper engineering training, and all CLF survivors have Endurance 1.
balance: CLF insert sentries no longer have unlimited ammo, instead
spawning with a random amount between 100 - 500 to simulate them having
been crashed for a while.
/:cl:

<!-- Write **BELOW** The Headers and **ABOVE** The comments else it may not be viewable. -->

# About the pull request

Replaces the current Queen sprite with a custom made one. Credits for the sprite goes to a list of devs and artists:

[CREDITS] 
medjic : For their work on a complete queen resprite for RUTGMC which served as the visual basis and guideline for this resprite 
Byte and mossymothling : For the first rough draft passes on the front and side sprites and the visual guideline for the backsprite
Zenith00000 / Zenith / zenith_zero : For completely redoing the head on the side sprite.
Buchse / Clockworkdoorhinge / Xenopera : For helping create the first draft of the ovipositor sack
Blundir: For refactoring xeno wound overlays entirely, allowing the larger canvas size to be used for wounds.
Drawsstuff : All final sprites including ovipositor and acid animations, directionals, and all states such as wounded, weeded, stunned, dead, and sleeping. 

All I did was organize the reviving of the project as well as putting together the pull request.

# Explain why it's good for the game

Queen's sprite is good, but doesn't represent just how unique a Queen is compared to her hive. She is massive and stands out visually, and this new sprite seeks to represent that in game.

Instead of just looking like a tall drone with a fancy crest, she truly looks nothing like any other xeno, as she should.

# Testing Photographs and Procedure



</details>


# Changelog

:cl:
imageadd: added a new queen sprite
/:cl:

<!-- Both :cl:'s are required for the changelog to work! -->

Adds supply radio to SL headsets (#9336)

# About the pull request

Adds supply radio to SL headsets. Fixes descriptions on headsets to fit
this new change.

# Explain why it's good for the game

QoL change that helps SL's be able to properly talk to Req outside of
having to play literal or figuratively telephone through radio backpack
/ asking FTL or SO to talk to req for you.

Since SL's that know what they are doing tend to need to talk to req a
decent bit, and FTL's already have this channel, I do not see the harm
of giving it to SL's as well.

Yes you can buy supply radio key or ask req, but it should be default
considering not having good comm channels like supply radio as a SL is
like not having medkits as a corpsmen or not having your tools for free
as a engineer.

# Testing Photographs and Procedure

<details>
<summary>Screenshots & Videos</summary>

N/A

</details>

# Changelog

:cl: Swagile
add: Gives SL radio supply channel access by default.
/:cl:

Accessory attaching fixes and reorganization (#9247)

# About the pull request

Title, as well as some refactoring with restricted accessory attaching 

# Explain why it's good for the game

Rather than painstakingly adding a list of restricted accessory
attachments to each goddamn armor type, accessory attachment limits are
now tied to the accessory itself in conjunction to its category.
This also means that there is now a limit to the number of patches you
can apply to your uniform, no more infinity stacking of patches.

Fixes ties and patches being unable to be attached by giving them their
own category.
Recategorizes the uniform storage accessories from
ACCESSORY_SLOT_UTILITY to ACCESSORY_SLOT_STORAGE
Stethoscopes are now categorized as ACCESSORY_SLOT_UTILITY for better
readability

Cleans up the valid_accessory list from every instance that it's on, no
more redundant listings when a goddamn parent actually has a list of all
you need.
As a result, accessory attaching is somewhat expanded, and you can now
attach patches onto armors although it may look ugly padless and
sleeveless.

#9244 is still good to keep up, but ideally future implementations of
accessories should not at all make use of ACCESSORY_SLOT_DEFAULT and
should be in its own category or within an existing one.

# Testing Photographs and Procedure

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3c77cf08-5b3a-4621-9967-9d5871b6add8)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3aa718c9-4e39-4083-957c-b96c37b34ecc)

# Changelog
:cl:
add: You can now attach patches on armor; this might look ugly if you
are sleeveless and padless, however.
add: Nearly every exosuit clothing can now hold accessories.
fix: Accessories now have a limit to them, typically only 1 but some
items can go up to 4.
code: Cleans up the valid accessory lists.
refactor: Defuncts restricted_accessory_slots on clothings for a more
intuitive per accessory slot limit dependent on accessory category.
/:cl:

---------

Co-authored-by: Drathek <76988376+Drulikar@users.noreply.github.com>

Changes the Jungle Moon hunting grounds map (#9292)

# About the pull request

- Updates the Jungle Moon hunting ground map to be closer in and
claustrophobic to dissuade people from rushing to certain areas of the
map on spawn.
- The Jungle Moon now has its own ambience and animal sounds, thank you
to the person who provided the animal sounds, you know who you are.
- Areas now have a hangable trait to allow for Yautja to hang up bodies
where there might not be a roof, but where it would be feasible for
bodies to be hung from trees IE. Jungle tiles.
- Fixes some upside-down tiles on LV-624.

<!-- Remove this text and explain what the purpose of your PR is.

Mention if you have tested your changes. If you changed a map, make sure
you used the mapmerge tool.
If this is an Issue Correction, you can type "Fixes Issue #169420" to
link the PR to the corresponding Issue number #169420.

Remember: something that is self-evident to you might not be to others.
Explain your rationale fully, even if you feel it goes without saying.
-->

# Explain why it's good for the game
The current map for the jungle moon is not bad. But it is the opinion of
many people that it encourages hunted individuals to rush to certain
areas of the map. Mainly, the open ground next to the camp. This version
of the map is more claustrophobic, with fewer open areas for people to
rush towards on spawn.

Fixing upside-down tiles on LV is long overdue.

where it would be feasible for them to be hung.
fix: Fixes some upside-down tiles on LV-624.
soundadd: Added New ambience for the Jungle Moon.
soundadd: Added three new random animal noises for the Jungle Moon.
maptweak: Updated the Jungle moon.
/:cl:

marsoc helmet removal shiva (#9228)

# About the pull request

removes marsoc helmet

# Explain why it's good for the game

#9066 

Survivors shouldn't be running around with light armor, marsoc helmet
with unlimited night vision, a MK1, a subdermal armor implant

The nvgs are so easy to get that there's no point contesting it, because
by the time you get there they already have that and a flamer so you die

survivors shouldn't have the same power as specialists, or marines
